revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 高祖神社 |画像 = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖1578 |ISO = JP-40 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 41.19 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 15|経度秒 =37.69 |祭神 = [[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]]
[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = 高祖宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Fukuoka city |ラベル位置= top }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[高祖山]]}}]] '''高祖神社'''（たかすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 通称は「高祖宮」{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。[[高祖山]]の西麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱。 * 主座：'''[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]]''' * 左座：'''[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]''' * 右座：'''息長足姫命''' - [[神功皇后]]。 === 祭神について === 高祖神社は『[[日本三代実録]]』に見える「高礒比咩神（高磯比咩神）」に比定されており、現在は見られないこの神を巡って、これまでに諸説が挙げられている{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 一説には、[[新羅]]渡来の[[アメノヒボコ]]（天日槍/天之日矛/天日桙）伝説と関連づけられ、特に高礒比咩神はその妻神に比定される{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。その根拠として、長野宇美八幡宮（糸島市川付）の社伝に関連伝承が見えるほか、『筑前国風土記』逸文『釈日本紀』巻10（述義6）伊覩県主祖五十迹手条所引『筑前国風土記』逸文。 - [{{NDLDC|991097/341}} 『国史大系 第7巻』]（経済雑誌社、1897年-1901年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）341コマ参照。では怡土県主祖の五十迹手は日桙の末裔と見える点、『筑陽記』では高祖を怡土県主の本貫地とする所伝が見える点、『[[日本書紀]]』『日本書紀』垂仁天皇2年是歳条。の渡来系伝承で「伊都都比古」という人物名が見える点が関連づけられる{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 また別説として、『[[和名抄]]』では[[筑前国]][[怡土郡]]に託杜郷（たこそごう、託社郷）が見えるほか、「筑前国嶋郡川辺里戸籍断簡」や『東大寺文書』では当地で宅蘇氏（たくそうじ）一族の居住が知られ、現在残る棟札にも「詫祖大菩薩」の記載が見えることから、これらと「高祖」・「高礒（高磯）」が関連づけられる{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}「託杜郷」『日本歴史地名大系 41 福岡県の地名』 平凡社、2004年。。宅蘇氏は詳らかでないが、「戸籍断簡」には「宅蘇吉志」として新羅官位由来の姓である[[吉士]]（吉志）を称して見えることから、渡来系氏族とも推測される{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 以上のほか、祭神に彦火々出見命（皇室祖先）を祀ることから「高祖」と名付けられたとする説（九州軍記）や{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}、「タラシ」の音から息長足姫命（神功皇后）を高礒比咩神に比定する説もある{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建は不詳{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。伝承では、古くは大下の地に鎮座したが、[[建久]]8年（[[1197年]]）に[[原田種直]]が当地に入るに際して、高祖神社宮司の上原氏と姻戚関係を結ぶとともに、[[高祖山]]の[[怡土城]]跡に[[高祖城]]を築城してその麓に高祖神社を移したという{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。また、古くは怡土城の鎮守神として祀られたとする伝承もある{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 糸島地方は『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[伊都国]]の比定地で（[[曽根遺跡群]]）、[[古墳時代]]にも古墳の密集地域として知られるが、一方で渡来系氏族による製鉄遺跡も認められており、上古の祭祀の性格については古来伝統祭祀と渡来系祭祀の両面で諸説がある{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では、『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）条『日本三代実録』元慶元年（877年）9月25日条。に「高礒比咩神（高磯比咩神）」が正六位上から従五位下に昇叙された旨の記事が見え、同神は当社に比定される（文献上初見）{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。しかし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載がないため、いわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる。 [[中世]]期には当地領主の[[原田氏]]から崇敬され、棟札によれば、[[永正]]4年（[[1507年]]）に原田興種が「怡土庄鎮守高祖山大菩薩御社」の社殿を造営している{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）には[[原田隆種]]が、[[元亀]]3年（[[1527年]]）には[[原田親種]]が、怡土郡一宮の「詫祖大菩薩」の宝殿を再興した{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。また古文書によれば、[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）に原田了栄（[[原田隆種]]）が志摩郡から5町を当社に寄進し、天正9年（[[1581年]]）に治木平衛門が五郎丸から1反半を寄進した{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。さらに天正10年（[[1582年]]）には「高祖大菩薩」の祠官が[[伊勢神宮]]への参詣時に[[吉田兼見]]を訪問したことや、天正15年（[[1587年]]）には[[原田信種]]が連歌千句・能五番の奉納を誓ったことが知られる{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。 [[File:Kuroda Mituyuki.jpg|thumb|160px|right|{{center|[[黒田光之]]}}{{small|[[福岡藩]]3代藩主。現在見る社殿を改築。}}]] [[江戸時代]]に[[福岡藩]]の領地となったのちも当社は藩主の黒田家から崇敬され、[[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）に3代藩主の[[黒田光之]]が社殿を改築し、[[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）に4代藩主の黒田綱政が石鳥居を造営している{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。また[[寛政]]6年（[[1794年]]）には、9代藩主の[[黒田斉隆]]が領内巡検の際に参拝した。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）11月に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。その後、[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）11月に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定され、大正15年（[[1926年]]）6月29日に[[県社]]に昇格している。 戦後、[[平成]]25-28年（[[2013年|2013]]-[[2016年]]）には本殿の修理工事が実施されている（平成の大修理）。 == 境内 == [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（左）・拝殿（右）
（福岡県指定文化財）}}]] '''本殿'''は、[[江戸時代]]の造営。三間社[[流造]]で、屋根は[[檜皮葺]]。棟札等によれば、[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）に[[原田隆種]]が造営、[[元亀]]3年（[[1572年]]）に[[原田親種]]が修理、[[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）に[[福岡藩|福岡藩主]]の[[黒田光之]]が改築したことが知られる。現在の本殿は寛文2年の工事によるものが基本とされ（柱材にはそれ以前のものを再利用か）、[[平成]]25-28年（[[2013年|2013]]-[[2016年]]）には修理工事が実施されている。この本殿は福岡県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 本殿前に建てられている'''拝殿'''は、江戸時代の造営。桁行3間・梁間3間、[[入母屋造]]で、屋根は瓦葺。天井は中央部が格天井、周囲が化粧小屋裏になる。棟札写によれば、[[享保]]16年（[[1731年]]）の造営とされる。この拝殿は福岡県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 本殿・拝殿の下段に建てられている'''石鳥居'''は、江戸時代の造営。[[花崗岩]]製の[[明神鳥居]]で、高さは約5メートル。柱の刻銘によれば、[[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）の福岡藩主の[[黒田綱政]]の寄進。この石鳥居は福岡県指定有形文化財（本殿・拝殿の附指定）に指定されている。そのほか、鳥居前の石段も福岡県指定有形文化財（本殿・拝殿の附指定）に指定されている。 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|境内 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|鳥居（福岡県指定文化財：附指定） File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|石段（福岡県指定文化財：附指定） File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), entrance.jpg|境内入り口 == 摂末社 == * 徳満神社 - 祭神：大名持神・少彦名神・保食神、例祭：9月13日神社由緒書。。 == 祭事 == 高祖神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 元旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 春大祭（[[4月26日]]） - 高祖神楽奉納。 * 夏大祭・千度潮井（6月第4日曜） - 前日夜に輪越し。 * 風止祭（[[9月1日]]） * 徳満宮大祭（[[9月13日]]） * 秋大祭（[[10月26日]]） - 前日夜に高祖夜神楽奉納。 * 神立祭（[[10月31日]]） * 神待祭（[[11月30日]]） * 大麻頒布祭（12月上旬） 春と秋の年2回には、'''高祖神楽'''が神楽殿で奉納される。[[応仁]]元年（[[1467年]]）に高祖城主の原田種親が京都から「京の能神楽」を伝えたものといわれ、江戸時代までは神職により、明治以降は地元民によって催される。舞神楽・面神楽の2種類12番があり、舞神楽は神楽面無しの音舞で、面神楽は面を着けての神話物語の演技になる。この高祖神楽は福岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 福岡県指定文化財＜－－令和5年６月に国の重要文化財に指定される === * 有形文化 ** 高祖神社 本殿・拝殿 2棟（附 石造明神鳥居1基、鳥居正面石段1基、棟札8枚、板札3枚）（建造物） - 2012年（平成24年）3月26日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.fukuoka.lg.jp/uploaded/life/65929_14340969_misc.pdf 平成24年3月26日福岡県公報]}}より福岡県教育委員会告示第4号。[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/010/bunkazai1.html 有形文化財一覧]（糸島市ホームページ）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 高祖神楽 - 1971年（昭和46年）5月19日に旧前原町指定民俗文化財に指定境内説明板。、1981年（昭和56年）3月5日に福岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定[http://www.fsg.pref.fukuoka.jp/bunka/contents_detail.asp?table=t68&fields=f2475&id=2222-8&bunrui=kenn_minz_mumi_mumi-kenn_minz_mumi_kiro 高祖神楽]（ふくおか社会教育ネットワーク「福岡県の文化財」）。[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/020/bunkazai2.html 無形民俗文化財一覧]（糸島市ホームページ）。。 本殿は[[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）に国の[[重要文化財]]への指定が答申されている（官報告示を経て正式指定となる）[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/93900401.html 文化審議会の答申（国宝・重要文化財（建造物）の指定）]（文化庁報道発表、2023年6月23日）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖1578 '''交通アクセス''' * [[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[筑肥線]] [[周船寺駅]]から ** バス：コミュニティバス（川原線）で「高祖」バス停下車（下車後徒歩約5分）[http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 高祖神楽]（糸島市観光協会）。 ** 徒歩：約30分{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}} '''周辺''' * [[高祖山]] ** [[高祖城]] ** [[怡土城]] - 国の史跡。 * 三雲・井原遺跡 - 国の史跡。伊都国王都の比定地。 * [[曽根遺跡群]] - 国の史跡。伊都国王墓の[[平原遺跡|平原墳丘墓]]を包含。 * [[伊都国歴史博物館]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{Reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板（高祖神社設置板、糸島市教育委員会設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=高祖神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/586}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）586コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=高祖神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 41 福岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490417|ref={{Harvid|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=奥野正男|authorlink=奥野正男|year=2000|chapter=高祖神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 1 九州 <新装復刊版>|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560025010|ref={{Harvid|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 高祖神楽] - 糸島市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たかすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] [[Category:糸島市の建築物]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 高祖神社 |画像 = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖1578 |ISO = JP-40 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 41.19 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 15|経度秒 =37.69 |祭神 = [[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]]
[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = 高祖宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Fukuoka city |ラベル位置= top }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[高祖山]]}}]] '''高祖神社'''（たかすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 通称は「高祖宮」{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。[[高祖山]]の西麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱。 * 主座：'''[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]]''' * 左座：'''[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]''' * 右座：'''息長足姫命''' - [[神功皇后]]。 === 祭神について === 高祖神社は『[[日本三代実録]]』に見える「高礒比咩神（高磯比咩神）」に比定されており、現在は見られないこの神を巡って、これまでに諸説が挙げられている{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 一説には、[[新羅]]渡来の[[アメノヒボコ]]（天日槍/天之日矛/天日桙）伝説と関連づけられ、特に高礒比咩神はその妻神に比定される{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。その根拠として、長野宇美八幡宮（糸島市川付）の社伝に関連伝承が見えるほか、『筑前国風土記』逸文『釈日本紀』巻10（述義6）伊覩県主祖五十迹手条所引『筑前国風土記』逸文。 - [{{NDLDC|991097/341}} 『国史大系 第7巻』]（経済雑誌社、1897年-1901年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）341コマ参照。では怡土県主祖の五十迹手は日桙の末裔と見える点、『筑陽記』では高祖を怡土県主の本貫地とする所伝が見える点、『[[日本書紀]]』『日本書紀』垂仁天皇2年是歳条。の渡来系伝承で「伊都都比古」という人物名が見える点が関連づけられる{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 また別説として、『[[和名抄]]』では[[筑前国]][[怡土郡]]に託杜郷（たこそごう、託社郷）が見えるほか、「筑前国嶋郡川辺里戸籍断簡」や『東大寺文書』では当地で宅蘇氏（たくそうじ）一族の居住が知られ、現在残る棟札にも「詫祖大菩薩」の記載が見えることから、これらと「高祖」・「高礒（高磯）」が関連づけられる{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}「託杜郷」『日本歴史地名大系 41 福岡県の地名』 平凡社、2004年。。宅蘇氏は詳らかでないが、「戸籍断簡」には「宅蘇吉志」として新羅官位由来の姓である[[吉士]]（吉志）を称して見えることから、渡来系氏族とも推測される{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 以上のほか、祭神に彦火々出見命（皇室祖先）を祀ることから「高祖」と名付けられたとする説（九州軍記）や{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}、「タラシ」の音から息長足姫命（神功皇后）を高礒比咩神に比定する説もある{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建は不詳{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。伝承では、古くは大下の地に鎮座したが、[[建久]]8年（[[1197年]]）に[[原田種直]]が当地に入るに際して、高祖神社宮司の上原氏と姻戚関係を結ぶとともに、[[高祖山]]の[[怡土城]]跡に[[高祖城]]を築城してその麓に高祖神社を移したという{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。また、古くは怡土城の鎮守神として祀られたとする伝承もある{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 糸島地方は『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[伊都国]]の比定地で（[[曽根遺跡群]]）、[[古墳時代]]にも古墳の密集地域として知られるが、一方で渡来系氏族による製鉄遺跡も認められており、上古の祭祀の性格については古来伝統祭祀と渡来系祭祀の両面で諸説がある{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では、『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）条『日本三代実録』元慶元年（877年）9月25日条。に「高礒比咩神（高磯比咩神）」が正六位上から従五位下に昇叙された旨の記事が見え、同神は当社に比定される（文献上初見）{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。しかし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載がないため、いわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる。 [[中世]]期には当地領主の[[原田氏]]から崇敬され、棟札によれば、[[永正]]4年（[[1507年]]）に原田興種が「怡土庄鎮守高祖山大菩薩御社」の社殿を造営している{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）には[[原田隆種]]が、[[元亀]]3年（[[1527年]]）には[[原田親種]]が、怡土郡一宮の「詫祖大菩薩」の宝殿を再興した{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。また古文書によれば、[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）に原田了栄（[[原田隆種]]）が志摩郡から5町を当社に寄進し、天正9年（[[1581年]]）に治木平衛門が五郎丸から1反半を寄進した{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。さらに天正10年（[[1582年]]）には「高祖大菩薩」の祠官が[[伊勢神宮]]への参詣時に[[吉田兼見]]を訪問したことや、天正15年（[[1587年]]）には[[原田信種]]が連歌千句・能五番の奉納を誓ったことが知られる{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。 [[File:Kuroda Mituyuki.jpg|thumb|160px|right|{{center|[[黒田光之]]}}{{small|[[福岡藩]]3代藩主。現在見る社殿を改築。}}]] [[江戸時代]]に[[福岡藩]]の領地となったのちも当社は藩主の黒田家から崇敬され、[[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）に3代藩主の[[黒田光之]]が社殿を改築し、[[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）に4代藩主の黒田綱政が石鳥居を造営している{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。また[[寛政]]6年（[[1794年]]）には、9代藩主の[[黒田斉隆]]が領内巡検の際に参拝した。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）11月に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。その後、[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）11月に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定され、大正15年（[[1926年]]）6月29日に[[県社]]に昇格している。 戦後、[[平成]]25-28年（[[2013年|2013]]-[[2016年]]）には本殿の修理工事が実施されている（平成の大修理）。 == 境内 == [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（左）・拝殿（右）
（福岡県指定文化財）}}]] '''本殿'''は、[[江戸時代]]の造営。三間社[[流造]]で、屋根は[[檜皮葺]]。棟札等によれば、[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）に[[原田隆種]]が造営、[[元亀]]3年（[[1572年]]）に[[原田親種]]が修理、[[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）に[[福岡藩|福岡藩主]]の[[黒田光之]]が改築したことが知られる。現在の本殿は寛文2年の工事によるものが基本とされ（柱材にはそれ以前のものを再利用か）、[[平成]]25-28年（[[2013年|2013]]-[[2016年]]）には修理工事が実施されている。この本殿は福岡県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 本殿前に建てられている'''拝殿'''は、江戸時代の造営。桁行3間・梁間3間、[[入母屋造]]で、屋根は瓦葺。天井は中央部が格天井、周囲が化粧小屋裏になる。棟札写によれば、[[享保]]16年（[[1731年]]）の造営とされる。この拝殿は福岡県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 本殿・拝殿の下段に建てられている'''石鳥居'''は、江戸時代の造営。[[花崗岩]]製の[[明神鳥居]]で、高さは約5メートル。柱の刻銘によれば、[[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）の福岡藩主の[[黒田綱政]]の寄進。この石鳥居は福岡県指定有形文化財（本殿・拝殿の附指定）に指定されている。そのほか、鳥居前の石段も福岡県指定有形文化財（本殿・拝殿の附指定）に指定されている。 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|境内 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|鳥居（福岡県指定文化財：附指定） File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|石段（福岡県指定文化財：附指定） File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), entrance.jpg|境内入り口 == 摂末社 == * 徳満神社 - 祭神：大名持神・少彦名神・保食神、例祭：9月13日神社由緒書。。 == 祭事 == 高祖神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 元旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 春大祭（[[4月26日]]） - 高祖神楽奉納。 * 夏大祭・千度潮井（6月第4日曜） - 前日夜に輪越し。 * 風止祭（[[9月1日]]） * 徳満宮大祭（[[9月13日]]） * 秋大祭（[[10月26日]]） - 前日夜に高祖夜神楽奉納。 * 神立祭（[[10月31日]]） * 神待祭（[[11月30日]]） * 大麻頒布祭（12月上旬） 春と秋の年2回には、'''高祖神楽'''が神楽殿で奉納される。[[応仁]]元年（[[1467年]]）に高祖城主の原田種親が京都から「京の能神楽」を伝えたものといわれ、江戸時代までは神職により、明治以降は地元民によって催される。舞神楽・面神楽の2種類12番があり、舞神楽は神楽面無しの音舞で、面神楽は面を着けての神話物語の演技になる。この高祖神楽は福岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 福岡県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 高祖神社 本殿・拝殿 2棟（附 石造明神鳥居1基、鳥居正面石段1基、棟札8枚、板札3枚）（建造物） - 2012年（平成24年）3月26日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.fukuoka.lg.jp/uploaded/life/65929_14340969_misc.pdf 平成24年3月26日福岡県公報]}}より福岡県教育委員会告示第4号。[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/010/bunkazai1.html 有形文化財一覧]（糸島市ホームページ）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 高祖神楽 - 1971年（昭和46年）5月19日に旧前原町指定民俗文化財に指定境内説明板。、1981年（昭和56年）3月5日に福岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定[http://www.fsg.pref.fukuoka.jp/bunka/contents_detail.asp?table=t68&fields=f2475&id=2222-8&bunrui=kenn_minz_mumi_mumi-kenn_minz_mumi_kiro 高祖神楽]（ふくおか社会教育ネットワーク「福岡県の文化財」）。[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/020/bunkazai2.html 無形民俗文化財一覧]（糸島市ホームページ）。。 本殿は[[2023年]]（[[令和]]5年）6月に国の[[重要文化財]]への指定が答申されている（官報告示を経て正式指定となる）[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/93900401.html 文化審議会の答申（国宝・重要文化財（建造物）の指定）]（文化庁報道発表、2023年6月23日）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖1578 '''交通アクセス''' * [[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[筑肥線]] [[周船寺駅]]から ** バス：コミュニティバス（川原線）で「高祖」バス停下車（下車後徒歩約5分）[http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 高祖神楽]（糸島市観光協会）。 ** 徒歩：約30分{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}} '''周辺''' * [[高祖山]] ** [[高祖城]] ** [[怡土城]] - 国の史跡。 * 三雲・井原遺跡 - 国の史跡。伊都国王都の比定地。 * [[曽根遺跡群]] - 国の史跡。伊都国王墓の[[平原遺跡|平原墳丘墓]]を包含。 * [[伊都国歴史博物館]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{Reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板（高祖神社設置板、糸島市教育委員会設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=高祖神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/586}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）586コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=高祖神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 41 福岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490417|ref={{Harvid|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=奥野正男|authorlink=奥野正男|year=2000|chapter=高祖神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 1 九州 <新装復刊版>|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560025010|ref={{Harvid|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 高祖神楽] - 糸島市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たかすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] [[Category:糸島市の建築物]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 高祖神社 |画像 = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿正面 |所在地 = [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖1578 |ISO = JP-40 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 32|緯度秒 = 41.19 |経度度 =130|経度分 = 15|経度秒 =37.69 |祭神 = [[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]]
[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]
[[神功皇后|息長足姫命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 三間社[[流造]] |別名 = 高祖宮 |札所等 = |例祭 = |神事 = |地図 = Fukuoka city |ラベル位置= top }} [[File:Mount Takasu (Fukuoka), enkei.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[高祖山]]}}]] '''高祖神社'''（たかすじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 通称は「高祖宮」{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。[[高祖山]]の西麓に鎮座する。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の3柱。 * 主座：'''[[ホオリ|彦火々出見命]]''' * 左座：'''[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]]''' * 右座：'''息長足姫命''' - [[神功皇后]]。 === 祭神について === 高祖神社は『[[日本三代実録]]』に見える「高礒比咩神（高磯比咩神）」に比定されており、現在は見られないこの神を巡って、これまでに諸説が挙げられている{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 一説には、[[新羅]]渡来の[[アメノヒボコ]]（天日槍/天之日矛/天日桙）伝説と関連づけられ、特に高礒比咩神はその妻神に比定される{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。その根拠として、長野宇美八幡宮（糸島市川付）の社伝に関連伝承が見えるほか、『筑前国風土記』逸文『釈日本紀』巻10（述義6）伊覩県主祖五十迹手条所引『筑前国風土記』逸文。 - [{{NDLDC|991097/341}} 『国史大系 第7巻』]（経済雑誌社、1897年-1901年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）341コマ参照。では怡土県主祖の五十迹手は日桙の末裔と見える点、『筑陽記』では高祖を怡土県主の本貫地とする所伝が見える点、『[[日本書紀]]』『日本書紀』垂仁天皇2年是歳条。の渡来系伝承で「伊都都比古」という人物名が見える点が関連づけられる{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 また別説として、『[[和名抄]]』では[[筑前国]][[怡土郡]]に託杜郷（たこそごう、託社郷）が見えるほか、「筑前国嶋郡川辺里戸籍断簡」や『東大寺文書』では当地で宅蘇氏（たくそうじ）一族の居住が知られ、現在残る棟札にも「詫祖大菩薩」の記載が見えることから、これらと「高祖」・「高礒（高磯）」が関連づけられる{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}「託杜郷」『日本歴史地名大系 41 福岡県の地名』 平凡社、2004年。。宅蘇氏は詳らかでないが、「戸籍断簡」には「宅蘇吉志」として新羅官位由来の姓である[[吉士]]（吉志）を称して見えることから、渡来系氏族とも推測される{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 以上のほか、祭神に彦火々出見命（皇室祖先）を祀ることから「高祖」と名付けられたとする説（九州軍記）や{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}、「タラシ」の音から息長足姫命（神功皇后）を高礒比咩神に比定する説もある{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建は不詳{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。伝承では、古くは大下の地に鎮座したが、[[建久]]8年（[[1197年]]）に[[原田種直]]が当地に入るに際して、高祖神社宮司の上原氏と姻戚関係を結ぶとともに、[[高祖山]]の[[怡土城]]跡に[[高祖城]]を築城してその麓に高祖神社を移したという{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。また、古くは怡土城の鎮守神として祀られたとする伝承もある{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。 糸島地方は『[[魏志倭人伝]]』に見える[[伊都国]]の比定地で（[[曽根遺跡群]]）、[[古墳時代]]にも古墳の密集地域として知られるが、一方で渡来系氏族による製鉄遺跡も認められており、上古の祭祀の性格については古来伝統祭祀と渡来系祭祀の両面で諸説がある{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}。 === 概史 === [[六国史|国史]]では、『[[日本三代実録]]』[[元慶]]元年（[[877年]]）条『日本三代実録』元慶元年（877年）9月25日条。に「高礒比咩神（高磯比咩神）」が正六位上から従五位下に昇叙された旨の記事が見え、同神は当社に比定される（文献上初見）{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。しかし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載がないため、いわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる。 [[中世]]期には当地領主の[[原田氏]]から崇敬され、棟札によれば、[[永正]]4年（[[1507年]]）に原田興種が「怡土庄鎮守高祖山大菩薩御社」の社殿を造営している{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）には[[原田隆種]]が、[[元亀]]3年（[[1527年]]）には[[原田親種]]が、怡土郡一宮の「詫祖大菩薩」の宝殿を再興した{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。また古文書によれば、[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）に原田了栄（[[原田隆種]]）が志摩郡から5町を当社に寄進し、天正9年（[[1581年]]）に治木平衛門が五郎丸から1反半を寄進した{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。さらに天正10年（[[1582年]]）には「高祖大菩薩」の祠官が[[伊勢神宮]]への参詣時に[[吉田兼見]]を訪問したことや、天正15年（[[1587年]]）には[[原田信種]]が連歌千句・能五番の奉納を誓ったことが知られる{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。 [[File:Kuroda Mituyuki.jpg|thumb|160px|right|{{center|[[黒田光之]]}}{{small|[[福岡藩]]3代藩主。現在見る社殿を改築。}}]] [[江戸時代]]に[[福岡藩]]の領地となったのちも当社は藩主の黒田家から崇敬され、[[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）に3代藩主の[[黒田光之]]が社殿を改築し、[[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）に4代藩主の黒田綱政が石鳥居を造営している{{Sfn|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}。また[[寛政]]6年（[[1794年]]）には、9代藩主の[[黒田斉隆]]が領内巡検の際に参拝した。 [[明治維新]]後、[[明治]]5年（[[1872年]]）11月に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。その後、[[大正]]4年（[[1915年]]）11月に神饌幣帛料供進神社に指定され、大正15年（[[1926年]]）6月29日に[[県社]]に昇格している。 戦後、[[平成]]25-28年（[[2013年|2013]]-[[2016年]]）には本殿の修理工事が実施されている（平成の大修理）。 == 境内 == [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（左、国の重要文化財）・
拝殿（右、福岡県指定文化財）}}]] '''本殿'''は、[[江戸時代]]の造営。三間社[[流造]]で、屋根は[[檜皮葺]]。棟札等によれば、[[天文 (元号)|天文]]10年（[[1541年]]）に[[原田隆種]]が造営、[[元亀]]3年（[[1572年]]）に[[原田親種]]が修理、[[寛文]]2年（[[1662年]]）に[[福岡藩|福岡藩主]]の[[黒田光之]]が改築したことが知られる。現在の本殿は寛文2年の工事によるものが基本とされ（柱材にはそれ以前のものを再利用か）、[[平成]]25-28年（[[2013年|2013]]-[[2016年]]）には修理工事が実施されている。国の重要文化財に指定されている。 本殿前に建てられている'''拝殿'''は、江戸時代の造営。桁行3間・梁間3間、[[入母屋造]]で、屋根は瓦葺。天井は中央部が格天井、周囲が化粧小屋裏になる。棟札写によれば、[[享保]]16年（[[1731年]]）の造営とされる。福岡県指定有形文化財に指定されている。 本殿・拝殿の下段に建てられている'''石鳥居'''は、江戸時代の造営。[[花崗岩]]製の[[明神鳥居]]で、高さは約5メートル。柱の刻銘によれば、[[元禄]]6年（[[1693年]]）の福岡藩主の[[黒田綱政]]の寄進。福岡県指定有形文化財（本殿・拝殿の附指定）に指定されている。そのほか、鳥居前の石段も福岡県指定有形文化財（本殿・拝殿の附指定）に指定されている。 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|境内 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|神楽殿 File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|鳥居（福岡県指定文化財：附指定） File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|石段（福岡県指定文化財：附指定） File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), entrance.jpg|境内入り口 == 摂末社 == * 徳満神社 - 祭神：大名持神・少彦名神・保食神、例祭：9月13日神社由緒書。。 == 祭事 == 高祖神社で年間に行われる祭事の一覧。 * 元旦祭（[[1月1日]]） * 春大祭（[[4月26日]]） - 高祖神楽奉納。 * 夏大祭・千度潮井（6月第4日曜） - 前日夜に輪越し。 * 風止祭（[[9月1日]]） * 徳満宮大祭（[[9月13日]]） * 秋大祭（[[10月26日]]） - 前日夜に高祖夜神楽奉納。 * 神立祭（[[10月31日]]） * 神待祭（[[11月30日]]） * 大麻頒布祭（12月上旬） 春と秋の年2回には、'''高祖神楽'''が神楽殿で奉納される。[[応仁]]元年（[[1467年]]）に高祖城主の原田種親が京都から「京の能神楽」を伝えたものといわれ、江戸時代までは神職により、明治以降は地元民によって催される。舞神楽・面神楽の2種類12番があり、舞神楽は神楽面無しの音舞で、面神楽は面を着けての神話物語の演技になる。この高祖神楽は福岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 高祖神社本殿（附 棟札6枚）（建造物） - 2023年（令和5年）9月25日指定令和5年9月25日文部科学省告示第108号。。 国の重要文化財への指定以前、本殿は拝殿とともに2012年（平成24年）3月26日に福岡県指定有形文化財に指定されていた。 === 福岡県指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 高祖神社 拝殿（附 石造明神鳥居1基、鳥居正面石段1基、棟札8枚、板札3枚）（建造物） - 2012年（平成24年）3月26日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.fukuoka.lg.jp/uploaded/life/65929_14340969_misc.pdf 平成24年3月26日福岡県公報]}}より福岡県教育委員会告示第4号。[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/010/bunkazai1.html 有形文化財一覧]（糸島市ホームページ）。。 * 無形民俗文化財 ** 高祖神楽 - 1971年（昭和46年）5月19日に旧前原町指定民俗文化財に指定境内説明板。、1981年（昭和56年）3月5日に福岡県指定無形民俗文化財に指定[http://www.fsg.pref.fukuoka.jp/bunka/contents_detail.asp?table=t68&fields=f2475&id=2222-8&bunrui=kenn_minz_mumi_mumi-kenn_minz_mumi_kiro 高祖神楽]（ふくおか社会教育ネットワーク「福岡県の文化財」）。[http://www.city.itoshima.lg.jp/s033/010/020/010/050/020/bunkazai2.html 無形民俗文化財一覧]（糸島市ホームページ）。。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' * [[福岡県]][[糸島市]]高祖1578 '''交通アクセス''' * [[九州旅客鉄道]]（JR九州）[[筑肥線]] [[周船寺駅]]から ** バス：コミュニティバス（川原線）で「高祖」バス停下車（下車後徒歩約5分）[http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 高祖神楽]（糸島市観光協会）。 ** 徒歩：約30分{{Sfn|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}} '''周辺''' * [[高祖山]] ** [[高祖城]] ** [[怡土城]] - 国の史跡。 * 三雲・井原遺跡 - 国の史跡。伊都国王都の比定地。 * [[曽根遺跡群]] - 国の史跡。伊都国王墓の[[平原遺跡|平原墳丘墓]]を包含。 * [[伊都国歴史博物館]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{Reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板（高祖神社設置板、糸島市教育委員会設置板） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=高祖神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088313/586}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 下』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）586コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2004|chapter=高祖神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 41 福岡県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490417|ref={{Harvid|高祖神社（平凡社）|2004年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一|editor-link=谷川健一|author=奥野正男|authorlink=奥野正男|year=2000|chapter=高祖神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 1 九州 <新装復刊版>|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560025010|ref={{Harvid|高祖神社（神々）|2000年}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 高祖神楽] - 糸島市観光協会 {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:たかすしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:糸島市の歴史]] [[Category:糸島市の建築物]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡県の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:福岡県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:都道府県指定無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | Name = Takasu Shrine | Image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | Address = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | Founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | Alternate Name = Takasu-no-Miya | Festivals = Spring Festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn Festival ([[October 26]]) | Map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == Festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various annual festivals, including: * '''Spring Festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' * Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' * Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. * From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Japan]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]]

File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == Festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various annual festivals, including: * '''Spring Festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn Festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' * Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' * Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. * From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Japan]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:original pages]]{{Check_everybodywiki}}[[Category:original pages]]

{{Shrine | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | Address = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | Coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | Deities = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | Founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | Alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | Festivals = Spring Festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn Festival ([[October 26]]) | Map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various annual festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' * Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' * Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. * From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Japan]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' * Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' * Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. * From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' * Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' * Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. * From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}

{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] {{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[category:kk]] It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture.
