{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:pages revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Takasu Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takasu Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Takasu {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:tier 1]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient burial mounds and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Japan]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}[[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}} [[Category:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:New]] [[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takasu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=draft}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=draft}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍|12=draft}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=draft}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=draft}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=draft}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[JR Kyushu]] {{ill|Chikuhi Line|ja|筑肥線|12=draft}}, [[Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takasu Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Prefectural cultural properties in Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Takasu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Takasu Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft}} and previously held the rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=draft}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=draft}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍|12=draft}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=draft}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=draft}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=draft}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = [[File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), shaden-1-2.jpg|280px]]
Main Shrine (Haiden) | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33|32|41.19|N|130|15|37.69|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima|ja|糸島市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|ja|福岡県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Ame-no-Hiboko|ja|天日槍|12=draft|12=draft}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=draft|12=draft}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=draft|12=draft}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|ja|福岡藩|12=draft|12=draft}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture.
