Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]], [[:en:Susenji Station|Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]], [[:en:Susenji Station|Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672660]] Wikidata name: Takasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = File:Takasu Shrine.jpg | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.544806|N|130.260519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[:en:April 26|April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]]. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[:en:April 26|April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]], [[:en:Susenji Station|Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:高祖神社]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]]. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]], [[:en:Susenji Station|Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672660]] Wikidata name: Takasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = File:Takasu Shrine.jpg | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.544806|N|130.260519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[:en:April 26|April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]]. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]]. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[:en:April 26|April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]], [[:en:Susenji Station|Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]]

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ([[:en:April 26|April 26]])
Autumn festival ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Itoshima, Fukuoka|Itoshima]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''[[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]]. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ([[:en:April 26|April 26]]) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ([[:en:October 26|October 26]]) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Chikuhi Line|Chikuhi Line]], [[:en:Susenji Station|Susenji Station]]. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]]

Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11672660]] Wikidata name: Takasu-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = File:Takasu Shrine.jpg | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.544806|N|130.260519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26}})
Autumn festival ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26}}) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26}}) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26}}) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line}}, {{ill|Susenji Station|en|Susenji Station|WD=Q5367396|lt=Susenji Station}}. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]]

It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26}}) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26}}) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line}}, {{ill|Susenji Station|en|Susenji Station|WD=Q5367396|lt=Susenji Station}}. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]]

Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}})
Autumn festival ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26|1=October 26}}) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145|1=Itoshima}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}}) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26|1=October 26}}) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}}, {{ill|Susenji Station|en|Susenji Station|WD=Q5367396|lt=Susenji Station|1=Susenji Station}}. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:pages]]

It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}}) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26|1=October 26}}) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}}, {{ill|Susenji Station|en|Susenji Station|WD=Q5367396|lt=Susenji Station|1=Susenji Station}}. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Buildings in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = File:Takasu Shrine.jpg | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.544806|N|130.260519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|1=Hoori}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}
Former {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = Unknown | Architecture= {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-bay style) | alternate name = Takasu-no-Miya | festivals = Spring festival ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}})
Autumn festival ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26|1=October 26}}) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145|1=Itoshima}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. [[co:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)]][[ja:高祖神社]][[zh:高祖神社]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Takasu Shrine | image = File:Takasu Shrine.jpg | location = 1578 Takasu, Itoshima, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|33.544806|N|130.260519|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Hoori|WD=Q1051960|1=Hoori}} | Status = {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}

Autumn festival ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26|1=October 26}}) | map = Fukuoka city | Label Position = top | prefecture= {{ill|Itoshima|WD=Q1011145|1=Itoshima}} }} '''Takasu Shrine''' (高祖神社, ''Takasu Jinja'') is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Itoshima, Fukuoka|en|Itoshima, Fukuoka|WD=Q1011145|lt=Itoshima|1=Itoshima}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is historically classified as a {{ill|Kokushi genzaisha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi genzaisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and previously held the Rank of a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine is also referred to as "Takasu-no-Miya." It sits at the western base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following three deities: * '''{{ill|Hoori|ja|彦火火出見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ホオリ}}''' (Hikohohodemi-no-Mikoto) - A prominent deity in Japanese mythology. * '''{{ill|Tamayori-hime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}}''' - Often associated with sea and purification. * '''{{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}''' (Okisaga Tarashihime no Mikoto) - A legendary empress in Japanese history. === Historical Interpretation of Deities === Takasu Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} as the "High Isobime God" (高磯比咩神). Scholars propose several theories about this deity, including connections to {{ill|Amenohiboko|ja|天日槍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hiboko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノヒボコ}}, a legendary figure from Silla, and interpretations related to the ancient inhabitants of the area, such as the Takuso clan. == History == The exact founding date of Takasu Shrine is unknown. Tradition holds that it was originally located in a lower area known as Ooshita and later moved to its current location at the base of {{ill|Mount Takasu|ja|高祖山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Takasu|qq=}} during the late 12th century by the local lord, {{ill|Harada Tanenao|ja|原田種直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harada Tanenao|1=Harada Tanenao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11409918}}. The shrine has long been associated with the Itoshima region, which is rich in ancient Kofuns and archaeological evidence of early Japanese culture. === Modern History === In the Meiji era, Takasu Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1872, promoted to a shrine receiving imperial offerings in 1915, and further elevated to a {{ill|Ken-sha|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ken-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1926. The shrine underwent major repairs, dubbed the "Heisei Great Repairs," between 2013 and 2016. == Structure and Features == The main shrine (honden) is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} structure with a cypress bark roof. It was built during the Edo period and underwent multiple renovations, including a significant reconstruction in 1662 under {{ill|Kuroda Mitsuyuki|ja|黒田光之|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Mitsuyuki|1=Kuroda Mitsuyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678571}}, the third lord of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}}. The front worship hall (haiden) and stone torii gate date back to the Edo period. The torii, constructed in 1693, and the stone steps leading up to the shrine are designated as cultural properties of Fukuoka Prefecture. File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), keidai-2.jpg|Shrine grounds File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), honden.jpg|Main shrine (Honden) File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), torii.jpg|Stone torii gate File:Takasu-jinja (Itoshima), ishidan.jpg|Stone steps == festivals and Events == The shrine hosts various festivals, including: * '''Spring festival''' ({{ill|April 26|en|April 26|WD=Q2532|lt=April 26|1=April 26}}) - Features traditional Kagura performances. * '''Autumn festival''' ({{ill|October 26|en|October 26|WD=Q2953|lt=October 26|1=October 26}}) - Includes Takasu Night Kagura, a cultural highlight. == Cultural Properties == * '''Nationally Designated Important Cultural Property''' ** Main shrine (honden) and related documents, designated in 2023. * '''Fukuoka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties''' ** Worship hall (haiden) and associated structures, including the torii gate and stone steps. == Access == * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Chikuhi Line|en|Chikuhi Line|WD=Q1042132|lt=Chikuhi Line|1=Chikuhi Line}}, {{ill|Susenji Station|en|Susenji Station|WD=Q5367396|lt=Susenji Station|1=Susenji Station}}. ** From the station, take the local community bus to the "Takasu" bus stop (5-minute walk) or walk approximately 30 minutes. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Takasu-jinja (Itoshima)}} * [http://www.itoshima-kanko.net/?contents-cat=%E9%AB%98%E7%A5%96%E7%A5%9E%E6%A5%BD%EF%BC%88%E3%81%9F%E3%81%8B%E3%81%99%E3%81%8B%E3%81%90%E3%82%89%EF%BC%89 Takasu Kagura Information] - Itoshima City Tourism Association. {{Shinto-navbox}} [[ja:高祖神社]] {{translated page|ja|高祖神社|version=97097325|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11672660}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Itoshima, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Itoshima]] [[Category:Prefecturally designated intangible folk cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:福岡県の Important Cultural Properties]]
